rationale: The citing note explicitly says the article extends prior compromise-effect research, “especially Kivetz, Netzer, and Srinivasan (2004),” into complex B2B contexts, which signals substantive engagement rather than incidental mention. The cited paper is the one that develops and tests the core compromise-effect models (CCM/NCCM/LAM/RAM), making it a plausible mechanism/theory anchor for the commentary.
